# Resource upgrade

This topic describes the steps to complete the Kubernetes resource types upgrade, when a new version of Consul requires it.

## Overview

Kubernetes resources for Consul provide a way to configure and manage Consul on Kubernetes clusters. 
Consul 2.0.0 provides new resource types that offer enhanced functionality and improved compatibility with OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) versions 4.19 and above. 
These new resource types are part of the `consul.hashicorp.com` API group.

## Requirements
- A Kubernetes with consul deployed

## Upgrade scenarios
### Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs not managed by the platform:

1. Kubernetes clusters that do not use the `TCPRoute` resources.
   
   a. User can optionally deploy their API Gateway on the New custom API Gateway launched in Consul 2.0.0 by enabling flags in the helm values files. Refer to Annexure 1 
   b. Post upgrade process. Refer to Annexure 2

### Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs managed by the platform. 
 Note:  1. Currently if the platform is managing the CRDs there is high possibility that Consul installation is failed. Here we solve the problem with Consul 2.0.0
        2. Thus assumption is your cluster is in v1 where Gateway CRDs are not managed by platform but in the next version it will be managed by platform, example OCP 4.18 to OCP 4.19

1. Kubernetes clusters that do not use the `TCPRoute` resources.
    
    a. User can optionally deploy their API Gateway on the New custom API Gateway launched in Consul 2.0.0 by enabling flags in the helm values files. Refer to Annexure 1.
    b. Post upgrade process. Refer to Annexure 2

2. Kubernetes clusters that use the `TCPRoute` resources.
    a. User will be mandated to use the New Custom API Gateway. Please refer to Annexure 1  for helm values.
    b. Upgrade process, refer to Annexure 3.

| Cluster Scenario                                                      | TCPRoute Usage                 | API Gateway Requirement                                                                                                              | Upgrade / Migration Action                                                                                                                   | Reference                |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **not managed by the platform** | Not using `TCPRoute` resources | Existing API Gateway can continue to be used. Users may optionally migrate to the new Custom API Gateway introduced in Consul 2.0.0. | Enable the required Helm flags to deploy the new Custom API Gateway if desired. Perform the standard post-upgrade process.                   | Annexure 1, Annexure 2/3 |
| Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform**     | Not using `TCPRoute` resources | Existing API Gateway can continue to be used. Users may optionally migrate to the new Custom API Gateway introduced in Consul 2.0.0. | Enable the required Helm flags to deploy the new Custom API Gateway if desired. Perform the standard post-upgrade process.                   | Annexure 1, Annexure 2/3 |
| Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform**     | Using `TCPRoute` resources     | Migration to the new Custom API Gateway introduced in Consul 2.0.0 is mandatory.                                                     | Deploy the new Custom API Gateway using the required Helm values. Follow the dedicated upgrade and migration procedure for `TCPRoute` users. | Annexure 1, Annexure 3/3 |

### Notes

1. If the platform manages Gateway CRDs, there is a high possibility that existing Consul installations may fail due to CRD ownership or reconciliation conflicts. Consul 2.0.0 addresses this issue.
2. This scenario is primarily applicable during platform upgrades where Gateway CRDs transition from being unmanaged to platform-managed, for example from OCP 4.18 to OCP 4.19.
3. The upgrade procedures assume an existing Kubernetes cluster with Consul already deployed.


## Annexure 1 : Helm Values required on API Gateway
Note:   1. For flag description, please check in Annexure 7.
        2. For the detail flow of upgrade check in Annexure 4.

### 1. Non-OCP
        a. Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **not managed by the platform** and not using TCPRoute.
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  # This will install upgraded Gateway CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    podSecurityContext:
                        enabled: false              # Set this to 'true', if platform needs elevated privileges to write access on PVC.
                    crds:
                        enableTcpRoute: false/true  # Set this to 'false', disables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                        consulapi:
                            enabled: true/false     # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```

        b. Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **not managed by the platform** and using TCPRoute.
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  # This will install upgraded CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    podSecurityContext:
                        enabled: false              # Set this to 'true', if platform needs elevated privileges to write access on PVC.
                    crds:
                        enableTcpRoute: false/true  # Set this to 'true', enables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                        consulapi:
                            enabled: true/false     # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```

        c. Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform** and not using TCPRoute.
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false # This will stop installation of Gateway CRDS.
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    podSecurityContext:
                        enabled: false              # Set this to 'true', if platform needs elevated privileges to write access on PVC.
                    crds:
                        enableTcpRoute: false/true  # Set this to 'false', disables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                        consulapi:
                            enabled: true/false     # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```

        d. Kubernetes clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform** and using TCPRoute.
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false # This will stop installation of Gateway CRDS.
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    podSecurityContext:
                        enabled: false              # Set this to 'true', if platform needs elevated privileges to write access on PVC.
                    crds:
                        enableTcpRoute: false/true  # Set this to 'false', disables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                        consulapi:
                            enabled: true/false     # Set this to 'true', this enables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```

        Note: User can enable the New Custom Gateway all the above scenarios. Please follow Annexure 3.

### 2. OCP
        a. OCP Clusters with Gateway CRDs **not managed by the platform** and not using TCPRoute.
           ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  # This will install upgraded Gateway CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: false  # Set this to true, if OCP Cluster version is 4.18 and needs to ugrade to 4.19+, Follow Annexure 6.
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false   # Set this to 'false', enables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: false      # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```
        
        b. OCP Clusters with Gateway CRDs **not managed by the platform** and using TCPRoute.
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  # This will install upgraded Gateway CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: false  # Set this to true, if OCP Cluster version is 4.18 and needs to ugrade to 4.19+, Follow Annexure 6.
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: true    # Set this to 'false', enables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: false      # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended if upgradeTo419From418 is false
                ```
        
        c. OCP Clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform** and not using TCPRoute.
           ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false  # This will install upgraded Gateway CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: false  # Set this to true, if OCP Cluster version is 4.18 and needs to ugrade to 4.19+, Follow Annexure 6.
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false   # Set this to 'false', disables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: false      # Set this to 'false', this disables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```
        d. OCP Clusters with Gateway CRDs **managed by the platform** and using TCPRoute.
           ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false  # This will install upgraded Gateway CRDS on v1
                    generateManifests: true         # This will generate manifests and applies them later on v1
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: false  # Set this to true, if OCP Cluster version is 4.18 and needs to ugrade to 4.19+, Follow Annexure 6.
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false   # Set this to 'false', disables controller to watch TCP GVK ( Group, Version, Kind)
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: true      # Set this to 'true', this enables the New Custom Gateway Controller, recommended
                ```

## Annexure 2: Consul Only upgrade, no platform upgrade: No 'New Custom Gateway'
### consul upgrade process to 2.0.0
1. With the above helm values from the annexure 1, set the values according to the scenario.
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>

## Annexure 3: Consul Only upgrade, no platform upgrade: with 'New Custom Gateway' as choice of gateway.
### consul upgrade process to 2.0.0
1. With the above helm values from the annexure 1, set the values according to the scenario.
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
3. At this point, you should see both gateways up and running, while your application is still **not accessible** through **new Custom API gateway** but accessible through other stable API Gateway.
4. To access the application through the **new Custom API gateway**, apply the serviceIntentions as intended. This is deliberately not done in our development.
5. Now user should be able to access the applicable through **new Custom API gateway**.
6. Update your DNS with the **New Custom API Gateway loadBalancer svc** and then delete the old stable API Gateway. Example: Route53.

## Annexure 4: Consul upgrade, platform upgrade to  version **Gateway CRDs not managed by the platform** and No 'New Custom Gateway'
### consul upgrade process to 2.0.0
1. With the above helm values from the annexure 1, set the values according to the scenario.
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
3. Perform platform upgrade.
### Note: 1. The application should be accessible, if not please run the helm upgrade setting generateManifests to false.

## Annexure 5: Consul upgrade, platform upgrade to  version **Gateway CRDs not managed by the platform** and with 'New Custom Gateway'
### consul upgrade process to 2.0.0
1. With the above helm values from the annexure 1, set the values according to the scenario.
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
3. At this point, you should see both gateways up and running, while your application is still **not accessible** through **new Custom API gateway** but accessible through other stable API Gateway.
4. To access the application through the **new Custom API gateway**, apply the serviceIntentions as intended. This is deliberately not done in our development.
5. Now user should be able to access the applicable through **new Custom API gateway**.
6. Update your DNS with the **New Custom API Gateway loadBalancer svc** and then delete the old stable API Gateway. Example: Route53.
7. Perform platform upgrade.

## Annexure 6: OCP specific upgrade from 4.18 to 4.19
### 1. With TCPRoute : case Annexure 1.2-OCP.d
    You can use below values.
        a. 
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  
                    generateManifests: true         
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: true  
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: true  
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: true/false     
                ```
        b. 
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true  
                    generateManifests: true         
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: true  
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false  
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: true      
                ```
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
3. This will generate the manifests and applies. Brings up 2 API Gateway: Old Stable and New Custom Gateway.
4. At this point, you should see both gateways up and running, while your application is still **not accessible** through **new Custom API gateway** but accessible through other stable API Gateway.
5. To access the application through the **new Custom API gateway**, apply the serviceIntentions as intended. This is deliberately not done in our development.
6. Now user should be able to access the applicable through **new Custom API gateway**.
7. Update your DNS with the **New Custom API Gateway loadBalancer svc** and then delete the old stable API Gateway. Example: Route53.
8. Follow the OCP documentation for platform upgrade here.

### 2. No TCPRoute
1. User can use the below values
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true 
                    generateManifests: true        
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: true  
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false   
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: false      
                ```
2. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
3. This will generate the manifests and applies the gateway objects under v1 version of the API group 'gateway.networking.k8s.io'
4. Follow the OCP documentation for platform upgrade here.
5. After the platform upgrade, aplication should be accessible, if not continue next steps.
6. User can use the below values
            ```yaml
                global:
                    installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false 
                    generateManifests: false        
                    openshift:
                        enabled: true
                        upgradeTo419From418: false  
                        crds:
                            enableTcpRoute: false   
                            consulapi:
                                enabled: false      
                ```
7. Perfom 'helm upgrade --install <release name> -n <namespace> -f <values.yaml>
8. Application should be accessible
                


## Annexure 7: New flag description for API Gateway.

1. installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true/false ( boolean )
    a. User can install the Gateway CRDs from Kubernetes.io; Or if the Gateway CRDs are managed by the platform, User can setb this 'false'.
    b. If Consul should manage the Gateway CRDs set this to 'true'
2. generateManifests: true/false ( boolean )
    a. if false, we dont run the job to generate the manifests for the Gateway and Gateway related Objects.
    b. if true, we generate manifests depending on the flags.

3. During the install:
    a. custom crds and gateway TCP crd enabling
    b. enableTcpRoute installs the TCP GVK under gateway.networking.k8s.io and enables the stable gatewaycontroller to watch for the resources.
    c. crds.consulapi.enabled install the CRDs under consul.hashicorp.com API group and enables the new custom gateway controller to watch for the resources.

   During the upgrade - manifests are generated.
    a. if consulapi.enabled is set to false; no manifests will be generated under consul.hashicorp.com API group
    b. if consulapi.enables is set to true; manifests will be generated and applied under consul.hashicorp.com API group\
    ```
        crds:
            enableTcpRoute: true
            consulapi:
            enabled: false
    ```
4. During the install:
    in this case ideally customer deploys the consul directly on the 4.19+ and thus is void.

   During the upgrade:
    This should be enabled when the customer plans to upgrade the OCP from 4.18 to 4.19+ versions.
    This will generate the manifests inaccordance to 2 cases along with enableTcpRoute:
    case 1: when upgradeTo419From418 is true and enableTcpRoute is false:
        generate the manifests only for gateway.networking.k8s.io and ignores the TCP watch
    case 2: when upgradeTo419From418 is true and enableTcpRoute is true:
        generate the manifests for both consul.hashicorp.com and gateway.networking.k8s.io ( the deleteion of TCP GVK is manual )
    
    ```
    upgradeTo419From418: false
    ```


5. During the install:
    For isOcpGreaterthan4_18: n this case ideally customer deploys the consul directly on the 4.19+ and thus is void.
   
    for crds.enableTcpRoute set to true; controller watches for the TCP
    for crds.enableTcpRoute set to false; controller does not watch for the TCp.

    for crds.consulapi.enabled set to true; controller watches for resources under the API group - consul.hashicorp.com
 
6. During the upgrade:
    If isOcpGreaterthan4_18 is false and enableTcpRoute is false:
        generate the manifests only for gateway.networking.k8s.io and ignores the TCP watch
    If isOcpGreaterthan4_18 is false and enableTcpRoute is true:
        generate the manifests only for gateway.networking.k8s.io and watches for the TCP
    If isOcpGreaterthan4_18 is true and enableTcpRoute is false:
        generate the manifests only for gateway.networking.k8s.io and ignores the TCP watch
    If isOcpGreaterthan4_18 is true and enableTcpRoute is true:
        generate the manifests for both consul.hashicorp.com ( all current GVK ) and gateway.networking.k8s.io ( no TCP )

    In any case, crds.consulapi.enabled install the CRDs under consul.hashicorp.com API group and enables the new custom gateway controller to watch for the resources.
    ```
    isOcpGreaterthan4_18: false
    crds: 
      enableTcpRoute: false
      consulapi:
        enabled: false 
    ```
Note: for more scenarios, please check in the values.yaml or contact support.

# API Gateway reconciliation

From 2.0 Consul, we reconcile only API Gateway/s controlled by Consul and also should contain the labels in the API gateway object. We check the object at two levels, the object should contain the label as below and the controller name of the object.

for old Stable gateway we should have labels as:
 ```
    apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
    kind: Gateway
    metadata:
    name: api-gateway # can be any
    namespace: consul
    labels:
        component: api-gateway # should be this
 ```

 for New Custom Consul Gateway we should have labels as:
 ```
    apiVersion: consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1
    kind: Gateway
    metadata:
    name: api-gateway-consul # can be any
    namespace: consul
    labels:
        component: api-gateway-consul # should be this
 ```
